markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Keep indexing useful when a single file/parser fails and record precise failure receipts. This MT belongs to CodeIndexingAndNavigation. Index code structure and expose precise backend navigation for agents.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Code navigation must use Handshake-managed parsers/adapters and store spans/citations in PostgreSQL.
Tree-sitter is the first parser family; LSP/SCIP-style precision is a Handshake-owned import/projection path, not an outside service requirement.
scope.expected_behavior: CodeIndexPartialFailureHandling leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CodeIndexPartialFailureHandling is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 commits 5d6490b0 (compiling foundation) + 79e1a7e4 (runtime proof); 43 lib + 11 integration + 1 nav-API tests green on real PostgreSQL under runtime-full,test-utils. Found+fixed impl-Type/struct-Type entity collision. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: REPAIR-QUEUE NOT IMPLEMENTED but presented as delivered. mod.rs:38/54/59 and engine.rs:24 describe MT-108 as 'partial receipts + repair-queue integration' and 'a repair-queue entry holds it (MT-108)'. The actual record_parse_failure (engine.rs:757-827) writes a `failed` source receipt + a `failed` knowledge_code_files row, and NOTHING ELSE - the comment at engine.rs:754 hedges '(when a repair hook is wired)', i.e. it is NOT wired. No enqueue to any ingestion repair queue occurs. The failure-recovery surface the contract claims (repairable files held for re-processing) does not exist; only a status flag + receipt are written. This is a prose-vs-code gap, not just a polish item.

Only CodeParseError is caught, not Io: index_code_file matches adapter.parse() Err -> record_parse_failure (engine.rs:251). But a binary / non-UTF-8 file never reaches the parser - std::fs::read_to_string in read_and_index (engine.rs:1101) returns CodeIndexError::Io, which propagates as a hard error. There is NO directory-runner in this module that catches Io per-file, so the 'one bad file never fails the run' guarantee covers grammar failures only; a single non-UTF-8/unreadable file aborts a `?`-propagating batch caller. mod.rs lists 'binary/invalid-UTF-8 input' as a handled failure mode, which is inaccurate for the read path.

No std::panic::catch_unwind around the tree-sitter FFI parse: parse() relies on tree-sitter never panicking. tree-sitter is C via FFI; an internal abort/panic is not a typed CodeParseError and would unwind past record_parse_failure, taking down the run. The 'never a panic' claim (parser.rs:27) is the grammar's behavior, not an enforced boundary.
evasion_probes: Verified the parse-error partial path IS real: a grammar init / no-tree failure is captured and the outcome carries failed=true with a receipt + code_file row (parse_status=failed); a tree WITH syntax errors that still yields symbols -> parse_status=partial and the run continues (engine.rs:276-280). This half of the contract works.

Tried to find a panic in the extractors on malformed input: extractors are pure Rust over the flattened node stream with Option-guarded text slicing; the delegate's malformed_source test shows a broken file returns root_has_error rather than erroring. No extractor panic found.

Confirmed record_parse_failure for CONFIG files reuses the same path with a placeholder language tag (engine.rs:857 maps to JavaScript) - the language tag in a config failure receipt is therefore WRONG (says javascript for a failed .toml), a minor receipt-accuracy bug.
evidence_checked: grep repair_queue across knowledge_code_index/** -> only docstrings, zero implementation

hardening_closed.note: Adversarial finding fixed + real-PG/unit test green (knowledge_code_index_tests 13 + nav_api 2 + 59 unit, 0 fail).

evidence: Hardened cf1b690d: Real durable knowledge_code_repair_queue (migration 0230) with enqueue/refresh/reopen/resolve; Io/non-UTF-8 + tree-sitter panic recorded not aborting; false repair-queue claim now true.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
